mapping.a_element: the scalar non-Gaussianity functional (squared 2-Wasserstein distance to the Gaussian)
mapping.b_element: the scalar minimum output p-Renyi entropy functional of a channel

mapping.a_element: linear combination of independent standardized sources
mapping.b_element: tensor product of two quantum channels fed an entangled input

mapping.a_element: strict inequality: non-Gaussianity of the combination < weighted sum of source non-Gaussianities
mapping.b_element: nonadditivity: minimum output entropy of the product channel < sum of individual minimum output entropies

brief_justification: The only overlap is the abstract shape 'a scalar functional is strictly sub-/non-additive when subsystems are combined,' which is too generic to count as shared machinery. The actual mechanisms differ decisively: A uses optimal-transport (Wasserstein) geometry with a deterministic CLT-driven inequality (mixing Gaussianizes) to identify an ICA unmixing matrix, whereas B constructs correlated random-projection quantum channels whose entangled inputs make minimum output Renyi entropy fail additivity -- different functionals, different combination operations (linear mixture vs. tensor-product channel), and different driving phenomena (transport/CLT vs. quantum entanglement + random matrices).
